history: Rachel has a history of recurring relationship problems with her husband Ivan and has experienced significant work-related stress, especially after being laid off recently. Her sister Emma often takes on a caring, supportive role, suggesting Rachel may seek reassurance from her. Rachel's father had a successful career and a strong work ethic, which has shaped her own high expectations and perfectionistic tendencies. She is currently looking for new employment and frequently compares her career aspirations to those of her spouse, which may contribute to her feelings of inadequacy and self-criticism.
unlovable_belief: I am bound to be abandoned
I am bound to be rejected
intermediate_belief: If I don't achieve as much as my husband, it means I'm not good enough and others will see me as a failure. I must always meet high standards to prove my value, otherwise I risk being judged or rejected. My success is the only way to feel secure about myself.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I can't match my husband's career success, it means I will never be good enough and there's no point in trying. My failures prove that I am hopeless and nothing I do will make a difference.
coping_strategies: Rachel tends to ruminate on her perceived shortcomings and compares herself negatively to her husband, which increases her distress. She seeks emotional support and reassurance from her sister Emma but avoids directly addressing her feelings with her husband. She may procrastinate or withdraw from job searching when feeling overwhelmed by self-doubt.
situation: Comparing personal career aspirations to those of the spouse.
auto_thought: I'm falling behind compared to Ivan. He’s successful and I’m just not measuring up.
behavior: I withdraw from conversations with Ivan and avoid discussing my feelings. I spend time ruminating about my failures and seek comfort from Emma instead of taking action on my job search.
